data.title: Taiwan Semiconductor's April sales surge on AI demand and rush orders amid US tariffs
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing's (NYSE:TSM) revenue for April surged 48.1% year-over-year to NT$349.57B amid growing demand for AI products.

TSM — which produces chips for some of the world's largest tech companies, including Apple (AAPL), Nvidia (NVDA) and AMD (AMD) — saw revenue jump 22.2% month-over-month, as in March 2025 the company had generated NT$285.96B.

Revenue from January to April soared 43.5% year-over-year to NT$1.189T.

The growth in April revenue underscored how electronics firms scrambled to buy essential components before global tariffs came into effect, according to a report from Bloomberg News.

However, earlier this week, it was reported that the Trump administration is set to alter export curb controls and end the Biden-era AI Diffusion rule.

On Tariffs, TSM Chief Executive C.C Wei had said on the earnings call in April that despite the potential uncertainties the company had not seen change in its customers' behavior and thus it continues to expect our full year 2025 revenue to increase by close to mid-20s percent in U.S. dollar terms.

During its first quarter results last month, TSM said that its business in the first quarter was impacted by smartphone seasonality, but was partially offset by continued growth in AI-related demand.

data.title: ASE Technology’s April revenue grows nearly 14% Y/Y
data.description:
• ASE Technology (NYSE:ASX) has reported its revised consolidated net revenues of NT$52.21B (US$1.59B) for April 2025, up +13.9% year-over-year but down -2.9% from March 2025.
  • Net revenues for ATM assembly, testing and material business were NT$31.31B ($953M), up 24.3% Y/Y.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Kwesst Micro System regains Nasdaq compliance
data.description:
• Kwesst Micro Systems (TSXV:KWE:CA) received a notification letter notifying the company that it has regained compliance with the minimum bid price requirement.
  • The letter confirmed that the company evidenced a closing bid price at or greater than the $1.00 per common share minimum requirement for 10 consecutive business days from April 24, 2025 to May 7, 2025.

data.title: Enel SpA reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Enel SpA press release (OTCPK:ENLAY): Q1 ordinary EBITDA: Є5,974 million (Є5,874[1] million in the first quarter of 2024, +1.7%)
  • Revenue of Є22.07B (+13.6% Y/Y).
  • Group net income: Є2,007 million (Є1,830 million in the first quarter of 2024, +9.7%)

data.title: European indexes rise amid hopes of Sino-U.S. trade talks, Frankfurt's DAX touches record high
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.51% to 8,575, snapping a two-day losing streak as investors digested upbeat earnings and fresh trade developments. 

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.73% to 23,522 to reach a new all-time high, extending Thursday’s gains and aligning with broader global market momentum. 

France (CAC:IND) +0.78% to 7,754. 

Construction activity in Sweden expanded by 2.2% Y/Y in March. Total orders received by Swedish industry climbed by 10.7% Y/Y in March.

Switzerland’s consumer confidence index stood at -42 in April.

Slovakia’s trade surplus narrowed to €406.4 million in March.

Retail sales in the Czech Republic rose by 3.4% Y/Y in March.

The annual inflation rate in Hungary slowed to 4.2% in April.

Household spending in Sweden fell by 0.4% M/M in March. Industrial production in Sweden dropped by 3.5% Y/Y in March.

Norway's annual consumer inflation rate edged down to 2.5% in April.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) edged 0.51% higher to 538.4, buoyed by improving global sentiment as investors show optimism about trade talks between China and the U.S. The new US-UK trade deal maintains the 10% U.S. tariffs on British goods introduced earlier this month and includes additional procurement contracts. Oil and gas, autos, and healthcare were the best-performing sectors, while insurance lagged.

The previous day, BOE decided to cut interest rates by 25 basis points, making it the fourth cut in this cycle. 

Coming up in the session: A lighter day on the economic front today.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 1 basis point to 4.38%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 6 basis points to 2.58%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 5 basis points to 4.59%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK).

data.title: Cardinal Energy GAAP EPS of $0.13, revenue of $149.77M beats by $48.75M
data.description:
• Cardinal Energy press release (OTCPK:CRLFF): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.13.
  • Revenue of $149.77M (+6.8% Y/Y) beats by $48.75M.

  • First quarter 2025 production of 22,005 boe/d increased by 1% compared to the same period in 2024.

  • Crude oil and NGL production both increased by 3% whereas natural gas production decreased by 10% resulting in 90% crude oil and NGL production, a 2% increase year over year.

  • Adjusted funds flow for the first quarter of 2025 was $62.2 million ($0.39 per basic and diluted share) compared to $52.8 million ($0.33 per basic and diluted share) in the first quarter of 2024. 

  • Average production for the first quarter was 22,005 boe/d, a 1% increase over the first quarter of 2024.

data.title: Entree Resources reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Entree Resources press release (OTCQB:ERLFF): Q1 operating loss was $0.6 million, compared to $1.1 million in Q1 2024.
  • The decrease was due to higher legal costs in 2024 for both commercial negotiations with OTLLC and Rio Tinto and the arbitration proceedings.

data.title: SoftBank GAAP EPS of ¥10.84, revenue of ¥6.54T
data.description:
• SoftBank press release (OTCPK:SOBKY): Q1 GAAP EPS of ¥10.84.
  • Revenue of ¥6.54T (+7.6% Y/Y).

data.title: Lundin Gold beats top-line estimates; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Lundin Gold press release (OTCQX:LUGDF): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.64.
  • Revenue of $356.35M (+57.2% Y/Y) beats by $16.99M.
  • Free cash flow of $171 million ($0.71 per share) was driven by strong gold production of 117,313 ounces, with 117,641 oz sold at an average realized gold price of $3,081 per oz, at low cash operating costs of $792 and all-in sustaining costs of $909 per oz sold. 
  • Gold sales totalled 117,641 oz, consisting of 78,552 oz in concentrate and 39,089 oz as doré.
  • The company generated cash from operating activities of $194 million and free cash flow1 of $171 million, or $0.71 per share, resulting in a cash balance of $452 million at March 31, 2025 following the quarterly dividend payment of $72.7 million.
  • EBITDA was $242 million while income from mining operations was $234 million
  • Mine production was aligned with mill availability which resulted in 403,221 tonnes mined at an average grade of 9.5 g/t.
  • Gold production was 117,313 oz which was comprised of 75,494 oz in concentrate and 41,819 oz as doré.

  • Outlook: With the process plant expansion project now complete, the Company is well-positioned to meet its production guidance of 475,000 to 525,000 oz and AISC guidance of $935 to $995 per oz sold.  Sustaining capital expenditures are expected to increase over the remaining quarters of 2025.

  • Based on the first quarter results, the company expects to increase the near-mine drilling program by 18,000 metres to a minimum of 83,000 metres to accelerate the definition of near-mine targets and the conversion drilling program from 15,000 metres to approximately 25,000 metres. A minimum of 108,000 metres of drilling are planned across the conversion and near-mine drilling programs for 2025.
